article_title: SANS, OWASP, and Cloud Security Alliance Issue Emergency Briefing as AI-Driven Exploits Compress Vulnerability Timelines From Weeks to Hours

content_preview.title: SANS, OWASP, and Cloud Security Alliance Issue Emergency Briefing as AI-Driven Exploits Compress Vulnerability Timelines From Weeks to Hours
content_preview.summary: A coalition of cybersecurity heavyweights warns that AI-driven vulnerability discovery has collapsed mean time-to-exploitation to under one day, urging CISOs to deploy AI agents against their own code immediately.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The SANS Institute, Cloud Security Alliance, OWASP GenAI Security Project, and security consultancy [un]prompted released a joint emergency strategy briefing on April 14 titled "The AI Vulnerability Storm: Building a Mythos-Ready Security Program," warning that AI-driven vulnerability discovery has compressed the mean time from disclosure to confirmed exploitation to less than one day in 2026, according to the [joint announcement](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/04/1...

recommendations: 1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong infosec article on SANS/OWASP/CSA emergency briefing on AI-driven exploit timelines.
editor_notes.source_verification: Sources captured in snapshots (GlobeNewsWire, CyberScoop, SecurityWeek). SANS/CSA/OWASP joint briefing April 14, mean time-to-exploitation collapse, Mythos 181 Firefox exploits, 60+ contributors, Jen Easterly/Bruce Schneier participation widely reported.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Coalition members and timeline compression data consistent across sources.
editor_notes.originality: No prior SANS/OWASP emergency briefing coverage.
